calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-23-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carole Scagnetti appeared by zoom for Bernice Lee (administrator).

The court stated that supporting time records were not provided for the ordinary fees, so it could not determine an appropriate amount for extraordinary services. Mrs. Scagnetti stated that this started out as a straightforward matter, and that issues developed and there was a great deal of contention between the heir and a person who claimed to have left personal property in the decedent's property. Mrs. Scagnetti also stated that the administrator had to fly out to Los Angeles to meet with this man because of the demand of the heir and that the administrator was not able to give her an exact number of hours. The court addressed the attorney's fees stating that the hours spent collecting declinations from the decedent's sister and the Public Administrator, and the services required to do the accounting are ordinary services. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jones. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-04-2024 Continued To Jul-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar May-01-2024 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-20-2024 Continued To May-01-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carole Scagnetti appeared by zoom for Bernice Lee (administrator).

Ms. Scanetti requested a 30-day continuance. Status on close of estate is continued to May 01, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel to file papers by April 17, 2024.

Judge: Stephen Murphy: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Singh: Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Continued To Mar-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Dec-19-2023 Continued To Feb-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nathan D. Borris, Esq. appearing for Bernice C. Lee (Petitioner);
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court addressed the petition and asked if anyone was appearing to object. No objections were raised by any party or counsel. The Court stated that Bank of America had filed two creditor claims for the following amounts: $9,223.00 & $9,131.00. The Court also stated that Bank of America was not objecting. Mr. Borris stated that he was not aware of the creditor claims until now. The Court stated that if a party did not appear this morning to object then they don't want to be appointed. The Court then granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with full authority (order signed in open court). Bond is set at $699,000.00. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee.

A status hearing on close of the estate is set on December 19, 2023, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ceclia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Lozado. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-22-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
